---
title: Installation
description: Install @justwant/env and set up validated environment variables with Zod.
icon: Download
---

## Install

<CodeBlockTabs defaultValue="bun">
  <CodeBlockTabsList>
    <CodeBlockTabsTrigger value="bun">Bun</CodeBlockTabsTrigger>
    <CodeBlockTabsTrigger value="npm">npm</CodeBlockTabsTrigger>
    <CodeBlockTabsTrigger value="pnpm">pnpm</CodeBlockTabsTrigger>
    <CodeBlockTabsTrigger value="yarn">Yarn</CodeBlockTabsTrigger>
  </CodeBlockTabsList>
  <CodeBlockTab value="bun">

```bash
bun add @justwant/env
```

  </CodeBlockTab>
  <CodeBlockTab value="npm">

```bash
npm install @justwant/env
```

  </CodeBlockTab>
  <CodeBlockTab value="pnpm">

```bash
pnpm add @justwant/env
```

  </CodeBlockTab>
  <CodeBlockTab value="yarn">

```bash
yarn add @justwant/env
```

  </CodeBlockTab>
</CodeBlockTabs>

Install a schema validator. Zod 4 is the most common choice:

<CodeBlockTabs defaultValue="bun">
  <CodeBlockTabsList>
    <CodeBlockTabsTrigger value="bun">Bun</CodeBlockTabsTrigger>
    <CodeBlockTabsTrigger value="npm">npm</CodeBlockTabsTrigger>
    <CodeBlockTabsTrigger value="pnpm">pnpm</CodeBlockTabsTrigger>
    <CodeBlockTabsTrigger value="yarn">Yarn</CodeBlockTabsTrigger>
  </CodeBlockTabsList>
  <CodeBlockTab value="bun">

```bash
bun add zod
```

  </CodeBlockTab>
  <CodeBlockTab value="npm">

```bash
npm install zod
```

  </CodeBlockTab>
  <CodeBlockTab value="pnpm">

```bash
pnpm add zod
```

  </CodeBlockTab>
  <CodeBlockTab value="yarn">

```bash
yarn add zod
```

  </CodeBlockTab>
</CodeBlockTabs>

**Note**: `@justwant/env` works with any `@standard-schema/spec`-compatible validator. The `@standard-schema/spec` peer dependency is bundled.

## Quick setup

```ts title="lib/env.ts"
import { createEnv } from "@justwant/env";
import { z } from "zod";

export const env = createEnv({
  vars: {
    PORT: z.string().transform(Number).pipe(z.number().int().min(1).max(65535)),
    DATABASE_URL: z.string().url(),
    NODE_ENV: z.enum(["development", "production", "test"]).default("development"),
    SECRET_KEY: z.string().min(32),
  },
});
```

```ts title="index.ts"
import { env } from "@/lib/env";

console.log(env.PORT);         // number
console.log(env.DATABASE_URL); // string
console.log(env.NODE_ENV);     // "development" | "production" | "test"
```

If any variable is missing or fails validation, `createEnv` throws an `EnvironmentError` at module load time — before your server starts.

## .env file

`@justwant/env` automatically reads `.env`, `.env.local`, `.env.{NODE_ENV}`, and `.env.{NODE_ENV}.local` in Node.js and Bun. No `dotenv` required.

```bash title=".env"
PORT=3000
DATABASE_URL=postgres://localhost:5432/mydb
SECRET_KEY=supersecretkeythatisatleast32chars
```
